DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api artifact-reference best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Variants Guideline

Documents all dimensions of visual or behavioral variation on a component. Each item in the variants array represents a single axis of configuration (e.g., 'size', 'emphasis', 'selected') and contains one or more values describing the options within that axis. A variant dimension with a single value represents a boolean/toggle configuration (e.g., 'full-width', 'icon-only'). A variant dimension with multiple values represents an enumerated set (e.g., size: small/medium/large).

Source: guidelines/variant.schema.json

3 definitions in this file:

  • variantValue
  • variantEntry
  • variants

variantValue

A single option within a variant dimension.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable value name (e.g., 'small', 'primary', 'selected', 'full-width').
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this value looks like, when to use it, and how it differs from the other values in this dimension.
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name (e.g., 'Small', 'Primary', 'Selected', 'Full Width').
tokensmap<string, string>optionalDesign token overrides applied when this value is active. Keys are token-purpose names scoped to the component (e.g., 'button-background', 'button-text-color'). Values are the token names or resolved values that apply for this variant value.
previewexample[]optionalVisual previews showing this variant value in isolation or in context.
useCasesuseCasesoptionalWhen to choose this value over the other values in this dimension.

References: richText, example, useCases

Example

[
  {
    "name": "primary",
    "displayName": "Primary",
    "description": "High-emphasis — the main action on the surface. Uses a solid, filled background. Limit to one primary button per surface.",
    "tokens": {
      "button-background": "color-action-primary",
      "button-text-color": "color-text-on-action",
      "button-border-color": "transparent"
    },
    "useCases": {
      "whenToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When the action is the most important on the surface — the one the user is most likely to take (e.g., Save, Submit, Confirm)."
        }
      ],
      "whenNotToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When a surface already has a primary button. Adding a second dilutes visual hierarchy.",
          "alternative": {
            "name": "secondary",
            "rationale": "Secondary emphasis maintains importance without competing with the existing primary action."
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "secondary",
    "displayName": "Secondary",
    "description": "Medium-emphasis — important but not the primary action. Uses a visible border and transparent background.",
    "tokens": {
      "button-background": "transparent",
      "button-text-color": "color-action-primary",
      "button-border-color": "color-action-primary"
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "ghost",
    "displayName": "Ghost",
    "description": "Low-emphasis — for tertiary actions, toolbar actions, or dense layouts where visual weight must be minimized.",
    "tokens": {
      "button-background": "transparent",
      "button-text-color": "color-action-primary",
      "button-border-color": "transparent"
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "danger",
    "displayName": "Danger",
    "description": "Signals a destructive or irreversible action. Use for delete, remove, or disconnect actions.",
    "tokens": {
      "button-background": "color-action-danger",
      "button-text-color": "color-text-on-action",
      "button-border-color": "transparent"
    },
    "useCases": {
      "whenToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When the action is destructive or irreversible — deleting a record, revoking access, removing a team member."
        }
      ],
      "whenNotToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When the action is not destructive, even if it feels important or urgent.",
          "alternative": {
            "name": "primary",
            "rationale": "The danger color is a strong signal reserved for destruction. Using it for non-destructive actions dilutes its meaning."
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  }
]

variantEntry

A single dimension of variation on a component. The name identifies the axis (e.g., 'size', 'emphasis') and the values array enumerates the options along that axis.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the variant dimension (e.g., 'size', 'emphasis', 'selected', 'full-width'). This is the axis name, not a value within the axis.
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this dimension of variation controls and how its values affect the component's appearance or behavior.
valuesvariantValue[]requiredThe possible values for this variant. A single-entry array indicates a boolean/toggle variant. Multiple entries indicate an enumerated variant. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve it for display.
Min items: 1
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name of the variant dimension (e.g., 'Size', 'Emphasis', 'Selected').
useCasesuseCasesoptionalWhen to use this variant dimension at all (as opposed to use cases on individual values, which address choosing between options within the dimension).

References: richText, variantValue, useCases

Example

[
  {
    "name": "emphasis",
    "displayName": "Emphasis",
    "description": "Controls the visual weight of the button. Determines background fill, border treatment, and text color to establish a visual hierarchy among actions on a surface.",
    "values": [
      {
        "name": "primary",
        "description": "High-emphasis — the main action on the surface.",
        "tokens": {
          "button-background": "color-action-primary",
          "button-text-color": "color-text-on-action"
        }
      },
      {
        "name": "secondary",
        "description": "Medium-emphasis — important but not primary.",
        "tokens": {
          "button-background": "transparent",
          "button-text-color": "color-action-primary",
          "button-border-color": "color-action-primary"
        }
      },
      {
        "name": "ghost",
        "description": "Low-emphasis — tertiary actions and dense layouts.",
        "tokens": {
          "button-background": "transparent",
          "button-text-color": "color-action-primary"
        }
      },
      {
        "name": "danger",
        "description": "Signals a destructive or irreversible action.",
        "tokens": {
          "button-background": "color-action-danger",
          "button-text-color": "color-text-on-action"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "size",
    "displayName": "Size",
    "description": "Controls the physical dimensions and internal padding of the button. All sizes maintain a minimum 44×44 CSS pixel tap target.",
    "values": [
      {
        "name": "small",
        "description": "Compact size for toolbars, table rows, and dense layouts."
      },
      {
        "name": "medium",
        "description": "The default size. Suitable for most contexts including forms, dialogs, and page-level actions."
      },
      {
        "name": "large",
        "description": "Touch-optimized size for mobile-first surfaces and marketing pages."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "full-width",
    "displayName": "Full Width",
    "description": "When active, the button stretches to fill the full width of its parent container. A single-value variant — the button is either full-width or it is not.",
    "values": [
      {
        "name": "full-width",
        "displayName": "Full Width",
        "description": "The button expands to 100% of its container width. Label text is centered.",
        "useCases": {
          "whenToUse": [
            {
              "description": "When the button is the sole action in a narrow container such as a mobile sheet, a card footer, or a single-column form."
            }
          ],
          "whenNotToUse": [
            {
              "description": "When multiple buttons appear side by side in a button group or dialog footer.",
              "alternative": {
                "name": "default width",
                "rationale": "Full-width buttons in a horizontal group force a stacked layout, which breaks the expected left-to-right reading order for action groups."
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    ]
  }
]

variants

Documents all dimensions of visual or behavioral variation on a component. Each item represents a single axis of configuration — a Button might have an 'emphasis' axis (primary/secondary/ghost/danger), a 'size' axis (small/medium/large), and a 'full-width' axis (a single boolean value). Multiple axes document orthogonal dimensions that can be combined independently.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"variants"requiredIdentifies this guideline as a variants specification.
itemsvariantEntry[]requiredThe variant dimensions. Each item documents a single axis of variation with its enumerated values. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display dimensions in this order.
Min items: 1

References: variantEntry

↑ Back to top

GitHub

On this page

  • variantValue
  • variantEntry
